---
title: "Mary"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#| include: FALSE

library(tidyverse)
library(rvest)
library(polite)
```


## Getting lists of parks
```{r}
robotstxt::paths_allowed("https://stateparks.com/")


session <- bow("https://stateparks.com/minnesota_parks_and_recreation_destinations.html", force = TRUE)

mn_parks <- read_html("https://stateparks.com/minnesota_parks_and_recreation_destinations.html")

parks_list <- html_nodes(mn_parks, "#parklink a")

parks_text <- html_text(parks_list)
```

## test - writing a formula
```{r}
form <- parks_text[1] |>
  str_to_lower() |>
  str_replace_all(" ", "_")

url <- str_c("https://stateparks.com/", form, "_in_minnesota.html")


# formula -- generates urls we can scrape (only in mn) --------------------------
url_list <- vector("character")
for (i in 1:length(parks_text)) {
  form <- parks_text[i] |>
  str_to_lower() |>
  str_replace_all(" ", "_")
  url <- str_c("https://stateparks.com/", form, "_in_minnesota.html")
  url_list <- c(url_list, url)
}
```


```{r}
#This works! Kind of? It only pulls 44 paragraphs, and even if the code was working perfectly it would leave out 2 state parks (total 52). If I do 51 or 52 to has a an error (open.connection(x, "rb") ?)
paragraph_list <- vector("character")
iteration <- 1
for (i in 1:50) {
  mn_park_ex <- read_html(url_list[iteration])
  paragraph <- html_nodes(mn_park_ex, ".clearfix+ .psbod")
  paragraph <- html_text(paragraph)
  paragraph_list <- c(paragraph_list, paragraph) 
  iteration <- iteration + 1
}
```

```{r}
url_list[5]
```
